page: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v4 is an EASv4 family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It includes 262 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most standard operations, with some notable bottlenecks in raw compute. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance falls in the bottom 10 percent, and multi-core performance is in the bottom 25 percent. However, memory bandwidth read-only tests for both small and large blocks score in the middle 50 percent, while large block write bandwidth is weaker, placing in the bottom 25 percent. General tasks such as gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests consistently register in the average tier.

Given the high memory density relative to the 8 dedicated vCPUs, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads rather than compute-heavy operations. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both perform in the average tier, making this configuration suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching, and moderate web serving. The hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy applications that require a large RAM footprint without needing high-tier multi-core processing power, though it is poorly suited for intensive LLM inference or heavy computational tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 physical cores, 8 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.525 GHz, and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The server includes 262 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no onboard GPUs. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, though its raw CPU compute performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling in the bottom 10 percent and 25 percent respectively. This memory-optimized profile is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and general web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over intensive CPU throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine with 8 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
128.0 GB of RAM providing 16.0 GB per core memory density
262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in database operations and memory bandwidth
Weak raw compute performance in single-core and multi-core benchmarks
Suited for in-memory caching, databases, and memory-heavy workloads
